text.title: Client-Conventions Guardrail Output Contract
text.summary: The methodology produces a single guardrail file at the agreed path (default AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md), versioned, evidence-backed, and treated as input to every agent task.

section.title: Required sections in the guardrail file

list.item: ## Machine-Enforced Rules — lint, format, type-check, pre-commit references. "Run X; do not duplicate rules here."
## Naming and File Conventions — file paths, class names, function names, test patterns, with evidence rows.
## Structural / Layering Rules — which layer may call which; where new code goes by category.
## Import Discipline — relative vs absolute, allowed external libs, banned imports.
## Dependency Policy — package manager, pinning, agent permission level for new deps.
## Branch + Commit Conventions — branch naming, commit message format, PR title format.
## Review Feedback Log — appended rules from rejected PRs, with date + evidence.
evidence: >= 2 citations (file paths OR PR comments).
confidence: high / medium (tentative; verify on first PR).

antipattern.reason: Violates r2. Becomes a hallucinated convention the agent enforces and reviewers reject.

antipattern.description: Rule contradicts the lint config (file says camelCase, eslint enforces snake_case).
antipattern.reason: Lint wins. Either fix the rule statement or fix the lint config — not both silently.

antipattern.description: Guardrail file covers formatting but lacks layering / import / dependency sections.
antipattern.reason: Violates r3 + r4. Produces functionally-correct code that PR review will reject for structure.

antipattern.description: Review-feedback-log absent or stale (no entries added in > 60 days while PR rejections still happen for convention reasons).

list.item: All 7 sections present and non-empty (or marked "N/A — justified").
No rule contradicts an active lint or formatter rule.
Review-feedback-log was updated within last 30 days (if PRs landed in that window).
Guardrail file is checked into the repo at the path the agent reads from.
